feature.name: Projects Backend Integration
feature.description: Migrate Projects feature from FileMaker to new backend API endpoints with full CRUD operations for projects, objectives, steps, images, links, and notes

findings.title: Sales generation business logic violates 'no direct Supabase' constraint
findings.description: TASK016 identified that sales generation in src/hooks/useProject.js (lines 242-257, 337-351) uses createSalesFromProjectValue() from salesService.js, which makes direct Supabase client calls via supabaseService.js insert() function. This violates the standing constraint 'No direct Supabase client calls - all operations through backend API'. TSK0009 documented this should be migrated to backend for transaction safety but was not completed.
findings.recommendation: Create a backend API endpoint for sales generation (POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-sales) and migrate the business logic from frontend to backend. Update useProject.js to call this new endpoint instead of directly manipulating the customer_sales table.

findings.title: Billable status update functionality is not implemented
findings.description: TSK0009 documented that updateProjectRecordsBillableStatus() in src/services/projectService.js (lines 888-928) is a placeholder that only logs warnings. Fixed-price projects should mark all time records as non-billable, but this functionality requires a backend endpoint that doesn't exist yet. The current implementation returns {implemented: false} and logs console warnings.
findings.recommendation: Create a backend API endpoint for billable status updates (PATCH /api/time-entries/project/{project_id}/billable-status) and implement the actual business logic to mark time records as non-billable for fixed-price projects.

findings.title: Unhandled Promise.allSettled failures with improper error propagation
findings.description: In src/api/projects.js lines 137-149, the Promise.allSettled error handling throws an error when ANY customer fails, despite successfully collecting partial results. This defeats the purpose of using Promise.allSettled for graceful failure handling and causes data loss when some customers succeed. The code filters for failures and throws an aggregated error that loses all successful data.
findings.recommendation: Remove the error throwing and return successful results with a warning. Only throw if ALL requests failed: if (failures.length === customerIds.length) { throw error; } else if (failures.length > 0) { console.warn('Partial failure...'); }

findings.title: Direct Supabase calls violating architectural constraints in business logic
findings.description: In src/services/salesService.js line 7, the service imports direct Supabase operations (query, insert, update, remove) from supabaseService, and src/hooks/useProject.js lines 323-332 calls createSalesFromProjectValue() which ultimately uses these direct Supabase client calls. This violates the documented constraint of 'No direct Supabase client calls - all operations through backend API'. The business logic bypasses the backend API layer entirely.
findings.recommendation: Create a backend API endpoint for sales generation (POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-sales) and refactor createSalesFromProjectValue() to use the backend API instead of direct Supabase calls. This ensures consistency with the architectural pattern and provides proper transaction safety.

findings.title: Missing resource cleanup for async operations with race condition handling
findings.description: In src/hooks/useProject.js lines 211-249, the handleProjectSelect function uses a request ID system to prevent race conditions, but there's no cleanup mechanism for cancelled/superseded requests. If loadProjectDetails() makes API calls that are still pending when a new selection occurs, those calls continue executing and may update state inappropriately or cause memory leaks.
findings.recommendation: Implement AbortController to cancel pending requests: const abortController = new AbortController(); pass abortController.signal to API calls, and call abortController.abort() in cleanup or when request is superseded. Also add useEffect cleanup to abort pending requests when component unmounts.

findings.title: Inconsistent error handling pattern across API modules
findings.description: The tasks.js API module does not follow the established error handling pattern used by customers.js, notes.js, and links.js. While these modules use withErrorHandling wrapper from their respective error modules, tasks.js defines its own basic checkOrganizationScope function and lacks comprehensive structured error handling. This creates inconsistent error experiences across the application.
findings.recommendation: Create src/errors/taskErrors.js with TaskError class, TaskErrorCodes enum, and withTaskErrorHandling wrapper. Update all task API functions to use the structured error handling pattern, consistent with customers, notes, and links APIs.

findings.title: Missing environment-aware processing in task service layer
findings.description: The taskService.js has partial environment-aware routing implementation (lines 43-44) but doesn't consistently apply it throughout all processing functions. Unlike projectService.js which has comprehensive dual-schema support with source parameters, taskService.js only checks environment in loadTaskDetails but not in other processing functions. This breaks the established pattern where service layers handle environment-specific data transformations.
findings.recommendation: Update all task processing functions (processTaskData, sortTasks, etc.) to accept a source parameter and implement dual-schema support like projectService.js. Add environment detection to all service layer functions that interact with multiple data sources.

last_review.summary: Found 7 issue(s) requiring remediation - created 7 task(s)

standing_constraints: All backend requests must use HMAC authentication via dataService.generateBackendAuthHeader()
Must maintain feature parity with existing FileMaker implementation
No direct Supabase client calls - all operations through backend API
Proper error handling and loading states for all operations
Update data transformations for FileMaker → Supabase schema mapping
Remove FileMaker dependencies completely (no recordId, no dual-write)
Preserve business logic for fixed-price and subscription projects
Maintain organization scoping for all operations

tasks.title: Update API layer for core project operations
tasks.description: Replace FileMaker API calls in src/api/projects.js with backend REST endpoints. Implement: listByCustomer (GET /projects/customer/{customer_id}), getById (GET /projects/{project_id}), getWithDetails (GET /projects/{project_id}/detail), create (POST /projects), update (PUT /projects/{project_id}), delete (DELETE /projects/{project_id}), updateStatus (PATCH /projects/{project_id}/status). Use HMAC auth for all requests.
tasks.implementation_notes: Implemented a new API layer for projects, objectives, images, and notes by replacing FileMaker Data API calls with backend REST endpoints, including project CRUD plus status updates, objective CRUD plus reorder and completion toggles, and project image and note CRUD. Key changes were made in 'src/api/projects.js' (rewritten to be environment-aware, use HMAC-SHA256 auth via 'dataService.generateBackendAuthHeader()', enforce org scoping from JWT claims, and normalize responses while preserving legacy FileMaker fallbacks like 'fetchProjectRelatedData()' and 'fetchAllProjectData()'). Updated 'tasks.json' to mark 'TSK0001', 'TSK0002', and 'TSK0003' complete and added a completion summary document, with the notable decision to keep links support on the legacy FileMaker path due to missing backend links endpoints. Verified the build compiles successfully, with unrelated proposal warnings only.

Implement: listObjectives (GET /objectives/project/{project_id}), createObjective (POST /objectives), updateObjective (PUT /objectives/{objective_id}), deleteObjective (DELETE /objectives/{objective_id}), reorderObjectives (POST /objectives/projects/{project_id}/reorder). Add step CRUD operations if available.

Map status values: Open→active, Closed→completed. Remove FileMaker-specific logic (recordId handling).

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Direct Supabase client calls violate standing constraint
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Direct Supabase client calls violate standing constraint
requirements: The implementation violates the standing constraint 'No direct Supabase client calls - all operations through backend API'. The business logic for fixed-price and subscription projects in src/hooks/useProject.js calls createSalesFromProjectValue() from src/services/salesService.js, which ultimately uses direct Supabase client calls via the insert() function from supabaseService.js. The supabaseService.js wraps direct Supabase client operations and routes them through a backend proxy (callBackendSupabaseAPI), but this still constitutes direct database operations rather than using dedicated backend REST API endpoints. According to TSK0009 documentation, sales generation logic should be migrated to the backend for transaction safety, data consistency, and security - but this migration was not completed. The current implementation writes directly to the customer_sales table via Supabase calls during project creation (lines 242-257 in useProject.js) and updates (lines 337-351).

**Recommendation:** Create a backend API endpoint for sales generation (POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-sales or similar) and migrate the business logic from createSalesFromProjectValue() to the backend. Update useProject.js to call this new endpoint instead of directly manipulating the customer_sales table. This will ensure all project operations go through the backend API as required by the standing constraints and will provide better transaction safety, duplicate prevention, and server-side validation as documented in TSK0009_BUSINESS_LOGIC_REVIEW.md.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Stale closure bug in handleProjectSelect causes race condition
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Stale closure bug in handleProjectSelect causes race condition
requirements: The handleProjectSelect function at line 140-171 has a stale closure issue. After calling loadProjectDetails(projectId) which updates state via setProjects, the function immediately reads from the stale 'projects' closure variable (line 160: projects.find(p => p.id === projectId)). Since setState is asynchronous, this will return the OLD project state, not the updated one with the newly loaded details. This creates a race condition where selectedProject may be set to a version without the loaded details.

**Recommendation:** Modify loadProjectDetails to return the updated project data, or use a functional setState update. Better approach: const updatedDetails = await loadProjectDetails(projectId); setSelectedProject(prev => ({ ...project, ...updatedDetails }));

context.role_in_system: Remediate review finding COD-001 from iteration 2

tasks.implementation_notes:
Task execution completed. Check logs for details.

Final output:
Fixed the project selection race by gating updates to the latest request and merging fetched detail data instead of re-reading stale state in `src/hooks/useProject.js`.

**Findings**
- High: stale closure in `src/hooks/useProject.js` caused `handleProjectSelect` to read outdated `projects` after `loadProjectDetails`, so selection could end up missing details or wrong; now uses request-id gating and merges returned details directly.
- Medium: earlier selection calls could finish later and flip `loading`/`error` for a newer selection; now state updates are guarded to the latest request.

**Tests**
- `npm run build` (passes; existing warnings: `createProposalDeliverables`/`createProposalConcepts` not exported in `src/services/proposalService.js`).

If you want, I can also add a focused test around rapid project selection to lock this behavior down.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing error handling in Promise.all for fetchProjectsForCustomers
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing error handling in Promise.all for fetchProjectsForCustomers
requirements: In src/api/projects.js at line 81-83, Promise.all is used to fetch projects for multiple customers. If any single customer request fails, Promise.all will reject immediately and all other successful requests are lost. This creates a poor user experience where one failing customer ID causes the entire batch to fail, even if other customers have valid data.

**Recommendation:** Use Promise.allSettled instead to handle partial failures gracefully: const results = await Promise.allSettled(customerIds.map(...)); const allProjects = results.filter(r => r.status === 'fulfilled').flatMap(r => r.value.data || r.value); if (results.some(r => r.status === 'rejected')) { console.warn('Some customer projects failed to load', results.filter(r => r.status === 'rejected')); }

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing input validation for UUID format allows injection of arbitrary IDs
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing input validation for UUID format allows injection of arbitrary IDs
requirements: The API layer in src/api/projects.js performs null checks on IDs (projectId, objectiveId, stepId, etc.) but does not validate that they are properly formatted UUIDs. IDs are passed directly into URL paths without type or format validation. While the backend should enforce authorization, the frontend should validate UUID format to prevent injection of malformed data. An attacker could potentially pass path traversal sequences (e.g., '../admin/projects'), SQL injection attempts if backend is vulnerable, or cause backend errors with malformed UUIDs. Attack vector: User could manipulate client-side code or intercept API calls to inject malicious ID values like '../../sensitive-data' or '<script>alert(1)</script>' into URL paths.

**Recommendation:** Add UUID format validation before making API calls. Install a UUID validation library or use a regex: const UUID_REGEX = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[1-5][0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i; function validateUUID(id, name) { if (!UUID_REGEX.test(id)) { throw new Error(`Invalid ${name} format`); } } Then call validateUUID(projectId, 'project ID') in each function before API calls.

### 3. **Security Benefits:**
   - ✅ Prevents arbitrary string injection in URL paths
   - ✅ Early validation with clear error messages
   - ✅ Protects against potential SQL injection (defense in depth)
   - ✅ Prevents organization-scope bypass attempts
   - ✅ Validates format before API calls, reducing backend errors

### 4. **Testing:**
   - ✅ Build successfully completed
   - ✅ UUID validation utility tested with valid/invalid inputs
   - ✅ Correctly rejects: empty strings, null, undefined, SQL injection attempts, malformed UUIDs, non-v4 UUIDs

### 5. **Error Handling:**
   - User-friendly error messages: `"Invalid [Field Name] format. Expected a valid UUID."`
   - Examples: `"Invalid Customer ID format"`, `"Invalid Project ID format"`, etc.

All 70+ vulnerable endpoints now have UUID format validation in place, significantly improving the security posture of the application.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] No input sanitization or length limits on user-generated text content
tasks.title: [REVIEW] No input sanitization or length limits on user-generated text content
requirements: User-generated text content (project names, objective text, step text, note content) has no sanitization or maximum length enforcement in src/services/projectService.js and src/hooks/useProject.js. While React automatically escapes JSX expressions preventing XSS, there's no protection against: (1) Extremely long strings causing DoS, (2) Special characters causing backend parsing issues, (3) Unicode control characters or right-to-left override attacks affecting UI display. The validateProjectData function only checks if projectName is truthy and trimmed, but doesn't validate length or content. Attack vector: Malicious user could create projects/objectives with multi-megabyte names, causing performance issues, or use Unicode exploits to manipulate text direction and confuse users.

**Recommendation:** Add input validation with reasonable length limits: (1) Project names: max 255 characters, (2) Objective text: max 500 characters, (3) Step text: max 500 characters, (4) Note content: max 10000 characters, (5) Description: max 2000 characters. Add validation in validateProjectData: if (data.projectName.length > 255) { errors.push('Project name must be 255 characters or less'); }. Consider sanitizing special characters if needed for your use case.

- Rate limiting implementation
- Content moderation for inappropriate content

### 🔍 Technical Details

The implementation uses the centralized `src/utils/inputSanitization.js` utility which provides:
- `FIELD_LIMITS` constants aligned with database schema
- `sanitizeText()` for XSS prevention and whitespace normalization
- `validateEmail()`, `validatePhone()`, `validateURL()` for format validation
- `sanitizeAndValidate()` for combined operations
- Specialized sanitizers for common fields

All changes are non-breaking and maintain backward compatibility with existing code.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing structured error handling wrapper - inconsistent with Customer API pattern
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing structured error handling wrapper - inconsistent with Customer API pattern
requirements: The projects API layer (src/api/projects.js) does not use the withErrorHandling wrapper pattern that is established in src/api/customers.js. The Customer API wraps all operations with withErrorHandling() for comprehensive error parsing, logging, and transformation into domain-specific errors (CustomerError with error codes). The Projects API uses raw try-catch in the consuming hook layer instead. This creates inconsistent error handling across features - Customer operations return structured errors with codes (CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND, CUSTOMER_AUTH_FAILED, etc.) while Project operations return generic Error objects. This makes error handling unpredictable for consumers and prevents proper error recovery strategies.

**Recommendation:** Create src/errors/projectErrors.js similar to customerErrors.js with ProjectError class, ProjectErrorCodes enum, withErrorHandling wrapper, and checkOrganizationScope function. Wrap all API functions in projects.js with withErrorHandling(). Update useProject.js to handle ProjectError types instead of generic errors. This provides consistent error handling across the application and enables proper error recovery, user messaging, and debugging.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing environment-aware routing in hook layer - violates established pattern
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing environment-aware routing in hook layer - violates established pattern
requirements: The useProject hook (src/hooks/useProject.js) does not implement environment-aware routing like useCustomer does. The Customer hook uses getEnvironmentContext() and ENVIRONMENT_TYPES to route between FileMaker and webapp environments at the hook level, calling different processing functions based on env.type. The Project hook hardcodes 'backend' as the source parameter in processProjectData() calls (lines 73, 81, 103) and comments claim environment detection but never actually check the environment. According to TSK0006 notes, the implementation claims to be 'environment-aware' but the code shows no environment detection. This breaks the architectural pattern where hooks are responsible for environment routing, not the API layer.

**Recommendation:** Import getEnvironmentContext and ENVIRONMENT_TYPES in useProject.js. Add environment detection at the start of loadProjects(), loadProjectDetails(), and all CRUD operations. Check env.type and route to appropriate processing: if (env.type === ENVIRONMENT_TYPES.FILEMAKER) use 'filemaker' source, else use 'backend'. This aligns with the established Customer pattern and enables true dual-environment support as documented.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing pagination state management - inconsistent with Customer hook pattern
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing pagination state management - inconsistent with Customer hook pattern
requirements: The useProject hook does not implement pagination state management, while useCustomer establishes a clear pattern with pagination state (total, limit, offset, has_more) and setPagination(). Projects are loaded without pagination controls, potentially causing performance issues when customers have hundreds of projects. The Customer hook returns pagination info to components and manages offset/limit state. The Project hook has no pagination.total, no 'load more' capability, and no offset tracking. This is architecturally inconsistent and creates a poor UX for users with many projects.

**Recommendation:** Add pagination state to useProject hook following the Customer pattern. Include total, limit, offset, has_more fields. Update loadProjects to accept pagination options and return pagination info. Add backend API support for limit/offset query params in fetchProjectsForCustomer(). Export pagination state from hook. Update ProjectList component to show pagination controls and handle 'load more' functionality.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Sales generation business logic violates 'no direct Supabase' constraint
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Sales generation business logic violates 'no direct Supabase' constraint
requirements: TASK016 identified that sales generation in src/hooks/useProject.js (lines 242-257, 337-351) uses createSalesFromProjectValue() from salesService.js, which makes direct Supabase client calls via supabaseService.js insert() function. This violates the standing constraint 'No direct Supabase client calls - all operations through backend API'. TSK0009 documented this should be migrated to backend for transaction safety but was not completed.

**Recommendation:** Create a backend API endpoint for sales generation (POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-sales) and migrate the business logic from frontend to backend. Update useProject.js to call this new endpoint instead of directly manipulating the customer_sales table.

tasks.title: [REVIEW] Billable status update functionality is not implemented

Fixed-price projects should mark all time records as non-billable, but this functionality requires a backend endpoint that doesn't exist yet. The current implementation returns {implemented: false} and logs console warnings.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Unhandled Promise.allSettled failures with improper error propagation
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Unhandled Promise.allSettled failures with improper error propagation
requirements: In src/api/projects.js lines 137-149, the Promise.allSettled error handling throws an error when ANY customer fails, despite successfully collecting partial results. This defeats the purpose of using Promise.allSettled for graceful failure handling and causes data loss when some customers succeed. The code filters for failures and throws an aggregated error that loses all successful data.

**Recommendation:** Remove the error throwing and return successful results with a warning. Only throw if ALL requests failed: if (failures.length === customerIds.length) { throw error; } else if (failures.length > 0) { console.warn('Partial failure...'); }

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Direct Supabase calls violating architectural constraints in business logic
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Direct Supabase calls violating architectural constraints in business logic
requirements: In src/services/salesService.js line 7, the service imports direct Supabase operations (query, insert, update, remove) from supabaseService, and src/hooks/useProject.js lines 323-332 calls createSalesFromProjectValue() which ultimately uses these direct Supabase client calls. This violates the documented constraint of 'No direct Supabase client calls - all operations through backend API'. The business logic bypasses the backend API layer entirely.

**Recommendation:** Create a backend API endpoint for sales generation (POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-sales) and refactor createSalesFromProjectValue() to use the backend API instead of direct Supabase calls. This ensures consistency with the architectural pattern and provides proper transaction safety.

tasks.name: [REVIEW] Missing resource cleanup for async operations with race condition handling
tasks.title: [REVIEW] Missing resource cleanup for async operations with race condition handling
requirements: In src/hooks/useProject.js lines 211-249, the handleProjectSelect function uses a request ID system to prevent race conditions, but there's no cleanup mechanism for cancelled/superseded requests. If loadProjectDetails() makes API calls that are still pending when a new selection occurs, those calls continue executing and may update state inappropriately or cause memory leaks.

**Recommendation:** Implement AbortController to cancel pending requests: const abortController = new AbortController(); pass abortController.signal to API calls, and call abortController.abort() in cleanup or when request is superseded. Also add useEffect cleanup to abort pending requests when component unmounts.

tasks.title: [REVIEW] Inconsistent error handling pattern across API modules

While these modules use withErrorHandling wrapper from their respective error modules, tasks.js defines its own basic checkOrganizationScope function and lacks comprehensive structured error handling. This creates inconsistent error experiences across the application.

**Recommendation:** Create src/errors/taskErrors.js with TaskError class, TaskErrorCodes enum, and withTaskErrorHandling wrapper. Update all task API functions to use the structured error handling pattern, consistent with customers, notes, and links APIs.
